Blood's density isn't three times that of water; it's primarily influenced by its cellular and protein content, not a fixed ratio.

questions.text: The drag force decreases as the speed of an object moving through fluid _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) Increases: This option suggests that the drag force decreases as the speed of an object moving through a fluid increases. This is not accurate. In most cases, the drag force actually increases as the speed of the object increases. At higher speeds, the object encounters more air molecules or fluid particles, leading to a greater collision frequency and an increase in drag force.

b) Decreases: This option suggests that the drag force decreases as the speed of an object moving through a fluid decreases. This is generally true. As the object slows down, the collision frequency with air molecules or fluid particles decreases, resulting in a lower drag force.

c) Remains constant: This option suggests that the drag force remains constant regardless of the speed of the object. This is not accurate. The drag force is dependent on the speed of the object. At different speeds, the drag force can vary.

d) Both B and C: This option suggests that the drag force decreases as the speed of the object decreases or remains constant. While option B is correct (drag force decreases as speed decreases), option C is incorrect (drag force does not necessarily remain constant).

questions.explanation: Drag force is a resistive force, which can only increase as forward force increases. This is in accordance with Newton's Law that every force has an equal and opposite force acting on it.

questions.text: Which of the following is the unit of pressure?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Kgms-¹ is the unit of momentum, not pressure.
Pressure = Force/AreaP = F/A= ma/m²= Kg (ms-²)/m²P = Kgm-¹s-²
Kgm²s-² is the unit of energy or work, not pressure.
This option is also incorrect. It is not the unit of pressure.

questions.explanation: Pressure is calculated as force per unit area. In this case, the correct unit of pressure is Kg m-1 s-2. This unit is derived from the formula P = F/A, where force is measured in Kg m s-2 and area in m2. This simplifies to Kg m-1 s-2, which is the unit of pressure.Option A is incorrect as it represents the unit of momentum, not pressure. Option C is also incorrect as it represents the unit of energy or work. Option D is not the unit of pressure either.

questions.text: S.I unit of viscosity is:-
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect. Although it uses 'N' for newton and 's' for second, it does not account for the correct dimensional analysis needed for viscosity.
This is correct. The dimensions of dynamic viscosity are expressed as force × time ÷ area, which translates to newton-second per square meter (N·s/m²), equivalent to pascal-second (Pa·s) in SI units.
Option C is incorrect. This expression represents a different physical quantity, as it inversely relates the time unit and incorrectly applies the area dimension.

questions.explanation: The correct SI unit for dynamic viscosity is newton-second per square meter (N·s/m²), which is equivalent to pascal-second (Pa·s). Dynamic viscosity is defined as the force needed to move a fluid layer over another per unit area and time, hence the dimensional analysis: force × time ÷ area. Option A incorrectly combines the units without proper dimensional consideration, while Option C misrepresents the relationship by inverting the time and incorrectly applying area.

questions.text: As the velocity of fluid flow increases, the pressure exerted by that fluid
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Bernoulli's principle states that in a fluid flow, as the speed of the fluid increases, its pressure decreases, and vice versa. This principle is a consequence of the conservation of energy in fluid flow. As fluid moves faster, the kinetic energy of the fluid particles increases, and this increase in kinetic energy is accompanied by a decrease in pressure. So, when the velocity of fluid flow increases, the pressure exerted by that fluid tends to decrease.

questions.explanation: Bernoulli's principle states that in a fluid flow, as the speed of the fluid increases, its pressure decreases, and vice versa. This principle is a consequence of the conservation of energy in fluid flow. As fluid moves faster, the kinetic energy of the fluid particles increases, and this increase in kinetic energy is accompanied by a decrease in pressure. So, when the velocity of fluid flow increases, the pressure exerted by that fluid tends to decrease.

questions.text: The property of liquid to which they resist their own flow is called.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The property of liquid to which they resist their own flow is called viscosity.
The rate of change of an object’s position in a particular direction. It has both magnitude (speed) and direction.
The property of a liquid surface that makes it behave like a stretched elastic sheet, caused by cohesive forces between liquid molecules.
The tendency of a body to resist any change in its state of rest or uniform motion.

questions.explanation: The property of liquid to which they resist their own flow is called viscosity. Hence, option A is correct.

questions.text: The principle of thin film is based on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Reflection of light refers to the bouncing back of light when it hits a surface. When light rays encounter a smooth and shiny surface, like a mirror, they reflect off at the same angle they hit the surface. This allows us to see objects and our own reflections. Reflection plays a big role in how we perceive the world around us. It's like light playing a game of "mirror, mirror".

The principle of thin film interference is based on the division of wavelengths of light. When light passes through a thin film, such as a soap bubble or an oil slick, it gets reflected and refracted. This causes constructive and destructive interference, resulting in different colors observed. The thickness of the film determines which wavelengths of light interfere constructively or destructively, leading to the colorful patterns we see. It's a beautiful phenomenon.

Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another. This bending occurs because light travels at different speeds in different materials. When light enters a medium with a different optical density, it changes direction, causing the light ray to bend. This phenomenon is responsible for various optical effects, such as the bending of a pencil in water or the formation of rainbows. It's pretty cool how light behaves.

False!The principle of thin film interference is based on the division of wavelengths of light. When light passes through a thin film, such as a soap bubble or an oil slick, it gets reflected and refracted. This causes constructive and destructive interference, resulting in different colors observed. The thickness of the film determines which wavelengths of light interfere constructively or destructively, leading to the colorful patterns we see. It's a beautiful phenomenon.

questions.explanation: The principle of thin film interference is based on the division of wavelengths of light. When light passes through a thin film, such as a soap bubble or an oil slick, it gets reflected and refracted. This causes constructive and destructive interference, resulting in different colors observed. The thickness of the film determines which wavelengths of light interfere constructively or destructively, leading to the colorful patterns we see. It's a beautiful phenomenon.

questions.text: The acceleration of a falling body in a fluid depends upon:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by its velocity. As a body falls, its velocity increases, and the drag force from the fluid also increases. This results in a change in acceleration over time as the body approaches its terminal velocity, where the drag force equals the force of gravity.

The viscosity of the fluid plays a significant role in the acceleration of a falling body. More viscous fluids, like honey, provide more resistance to motion, resulting in a different acceleration profile compared to less viscous fluids, such as air.

The density of the falling body affects its buoyancy in the fluid, which, in turn, affects the net force acting on the body. The acceleration of the body in the fluid depends on this net force, which is influenced by the density of the body.

The statement "All of the above" means that the acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by all these factors together, and no single factor alone can fully explain the complex dynamics of a body falling through a fluid.

questions.explanation: The statement "All of the above" means that the acceleration of a falling body in a fluid is influenced by all these factors together, and no single factor alone can fully explain the complex dynamics of a body falling through a fluid.Acceleration depends on velocity, density, radius, and viscosity.

questions.text: When a heavy coin fallsa short distance towards the ground it does not reach terminal velocity. Why is this so?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: The coin has not hit the ground
The weight of coin is equal to air resistance
The weight of coin increases as air resistance increases
The weight of coin is more than air resistance
options.explanation: In the case of a heavy coin falling a short distance towards the ground, it does not reach terminal velocity because the weight of the coin is greater than the air resistance it experiences. As the coin falls, the force of gravity acting on it (weight) is larger than the opposing force of air resistance. As a result, the net force on the coin remains non-zero, and it continues to accelerate until it hits the ground.

questions.explanation: In the case of a heavy coin falling a short distance towards the ground, it does not reach terminal velocity because the weight of the coin is greater than the air resistance it experiences. As the coin falls, the force of gravity acting on it (weight) is larger than the opposing force of air resistance. As a result, the net force on the coin remains non-zero, and it continues to accelerate until it hits the ground.

questions.text: Mass flow per second of the fluid is given by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: ρAv: This option represents the mass flow rate of a fluid. Here, ρ represents the density of the fluid, A represents the cross-sectional area through which the fluid is flowing, and v represents the velocity of the fluid. Multiplying the density, cross-sectional area, and velocity gives you the mass flow per second of the fluid.

Av: This option represents the volumetric flow rate of a fluid. Here, A represents the cross-sectional area through which the fluid is flowing, and v represents the velocity of the fluid. Multiplying the cross-sectional area and velocity gives you the volume flow per second of the fluid.

ρv: This option represents the momentum flow rate of a fluid. Here, ρ represents the density of the fluid, and v represents the velocity of the fluid. Multiplying the density and velocity gives you the momentum flow per second of the fluid.
Av/ρ: This option represents the inverse of the density. It does not directly represent a flow rate or a quantity related to fluid flow.

questions.explanation: 𝑀𝑎𝑠𝑠/𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑒 = ρ × 𝑉/𝑇 = ρ × 𝐴 × 𝑑/𝑇 = ρ × 𝐴 × 𝑣Where T is time, V is volume, A is area, v is velocity.Mass flow per second of fluid is given by: pAv

questions.text: A two meter high tank is full of water. A hole is made in the middle of the tank. The speed of efflux is;
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This speed is slightly overestimated. It would be correct if the hole were slightly lower in the tank.
This speed is too high and represents the theoretical maximum velocity if the hole were at the bottom of a 5-meter high tank.
Correct. Using Torricelli's theorem with the height of 1 meter results in this speed.
This speed is underestimated and would be applicable if the hole were higher up in the tank.

questions.explanation: The speed of efflux can be calculated using Torricelli's theorem, which states that v = √(2gh), where g is the acceleration due to gravity (9.8 ms-2), and h is the height of the fluid above the hole. In this case, the hole is in the middle of the tank, so h = 1 meter (half of the 2-meter tank height). Plugging these values into the formula gives v = √(2 × 9.8 × 1) = √19.6 ≈ 4.42 ms-1.Option A (4.9 ms-1) assumes a slightly higher height, Option B (9.8 ms-1) assumes the hole is at the bottom of a much taller tank, and Option D (3.75 ms-1) assumes a lower height than is given by the conditions of the problem.

questions.text: Conservation of mass of fluid flow leads to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Bernoulli's principle relates to the conservation of energy in flowing fluids, not mass. It explains how pressure, velocity, and height are related in a moving fluid.
A Venturi meter measures fluid flow rate by utilizing the principles of Bernoulli's equation, focusing on changes in pressure and velocity, not directly on mass conservation.
The equations of motion describe the behavior of physical objects under the influence of forces. They are not directly applicable to the specific conservation of mass in fluid dynamics.

The equation of continuity is a fundamental principle in fluid dynamics, expressing the conservation of mass. It states that the mass flow rate is constant from one cross-section of a pipe to another: A1V1 = A2V2.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is D) Equation of continuity, as it directly expresses the conservation of mass in fluid flow. This principle ensures that the mass of fluid entering a section of a pipe equals the mass leaving it, represented by the formula A1V1 = A2V2. Option A) Bernoulli’s equation, while significant, deals with energy conservation, not mass. Option B) Venturi meter is a device that applies Bernoulli’s principle to measure flow rate, not directly related to mass conservation. Option C) Equation of motion is irrelevant to fluid dynamics in this context, as it pertains to solid objects and forces.

questions.text: In a stream lined flow the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: a) ZeroThis option suggests that in streamlined flow, the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is zero. However, this is not correct. In streamlined flow (also known as laminar flow), the fluid particles move smoothly in parallel layers or streamlines. The velocity of the fluid at the boundary or in contact with the vessel is not zero; it is the same as the velocity of the fluid at other points within the flow.

b) Minimum but not zeroThis option suggests that the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is the minimum but not zero. This is the correct answer. In streamlined flow, the velocity of the fluid in contact with the vessel is minimum compared to the velocity of the fluid away from the boundaries. The velocity increases as we move away from the boundary and towards the center of the flow.

c) LargeThis option suggests that in streamlined flow, the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is large. This is not correct. As explained in option (b), the velocity at the boundary is minimum and gradually increases as we move towards the center of the flow.

d) PeripheralThis option suggests that in streamlined flow, the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is peripheral. This is not a relevant description of the flow behavior. Streamlined flow is characterized by smooth, parallel layers of fluid moving in a specific direction. The term "peripheral" is not used to describe the velocity in streamlined flow.

questions.explanation: b) Minimum but not zeroThis option suggests that the velocity of the liquid in contact with the containing vessel is the minimum but not zero. This is the correct answer. In streamlined flow, the velocity of the fluid in contact with the vessel is minimum compared to the velocity of the fluid away from the boundaries. The velocity increases as we move away from the boundary and towards the center of the flow.

questions.text: If an object of weight 90N in air, 60N ia..water and 20N in an unknown liquid, the specific gravity of liquid is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Review the calculation for specific gravity using the object's weight in different mediums.
This option is incorrect. Consider the buoyant force exerted by the unknown liquid.
This option is incorrect. Ensure the weight differences are calculated correctly for the specific gravity formula.
This is the correct answer. Specific gravity is determined by dividing the weight of the object in air by the loss of weight in water. Here, it is 90N / 30N = 3.0.

questions.explanation: Specific Gravity = (Weight in Air) / (Loss of Weight in Water)In this case, the weight in air is 90 Newtons, and the loss of weight in water is the difference between the weight in air and the weight in water, which is 90 Newtons - 60 Newtons = 30 Newtons.So, the specific gravity of the unknown liquid is:Specific Gravity = 90 Newtons / 30 Newtons = 3Hence all other options are incorrect

questions.text: Root mean square velocity of a gas having pressure ‘P’ and density ‘ρ’ is given by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: √(3P/ρ): This option represents the root mean square (RMS) velocity of a gas and is given by the equation √(3P/ρ). It takes into account the pressure (P) and density (ρ) of the gas.
3Pρ: This option does not represent the correct equation for the root mean square velocity. It is not a valid formula for calculating the RMS velocity of a gas.
3ρ/P: This option does not represent the correct equation for the root mean square velocity. It is not a valid formula for calculating the RMS velocity of a gas.

√3ρ/P: This option represents the correct equation for the root mean square velocity of a gas. The RMS velocity is given by the formula √(3ρ/P), where ρ is the density of the gas and P is the pressure.

questions.explanation: √3P/ρ: This option represents the correct equation for the root mean square velocity of a gas. The RMS velocity is given by the formula √(3ρ/P), where ρ is the density of the gas and P is the pressure.

questions.text: When fluid is incompressible, the quantity is constant.

questions.explanation: In an incompressible fluid, particles have a constant density. An incompressible fluid is defined as one whose density does not change on applying pressure; it's an ideal liquid and does not exist in real life.

questions.text: Water flows from a 6.0cm diameter pipe into 8.0cm diameter pipe. The speed in the 6.0cm pipe is 5.0m/s. the speed in the 8cm pipe is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct option. Using the continuity equation, A1V1 = A2V2, where A = πr2, the calculations show that V2 is indeed 2.8 m/s.
This option is incorrect because it does not satisfy the continuity equation. The calculation of V2 using A1V1 = A2V2 results in a different value.
This option is incorrect. A higher speed in the larger diameter pipe would violate the continuity equation, which requires the velocity to decrease when the cross-sectional area increases.
This option is incorrect as it significantly overestimates the speed. The continuity equation dictates that the speed must decrease as the diameter increases.

questions.explanation: The problem can be solved using the continuity equation, which states that A1V1 = A2V2, where A is the cross-sectional area of the pipe. For circular pipes, A = πr2, so:For the 6.0 cm diameter pipe, r1 = 3 cm, and for the 8.0 cm diameter pipe, r2 = 4 cm.Substituting these into the equation:π(3)2 × 5.0 = π(4)2 × V245 = 16V2V2 = 45/16 = 2.8 m/sThe correct answer is 2.8 m/s. questions.text: An Incompressible liquid flow along the pipe with area of cross section A1 and A2 with velocities V1 and V2 respectively. The ratio of the speeds V1 / V2 is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. It suggests the opposite of the correct relationship. The velocity ratio is inversely proportional to the area ratio.
This option is correct. According to the continuity equation, the product of cross-sectional area and velocity is constant for incompressible flow, leading to v1/v2 = A2/A1.
This option is incorrect. It incorrectly combines areas and velocities, which is not supported by the continuity equation.
This option is incorrect. The ratio of velocities does not depend on the sum of velocities and areas.

questions.explanation: According to the continuity equation for incompressible fluids, the flow rate (product of cross-sectional area and velocity) is constant. This means that A1V1 = A2V2. From this relationship, we can derive that V1/V2 = A2/A1, making Option B the correct choice.Options A and C incorrectly suggest relationships that don't comply with the continuity equation, while Option D presents a ratio that doesn't apply to the problem's context.

questions.text: Torricelli’s theorem be written as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option correctly applies Torricelli’s theorem, where the velocity is derived from the height difference of the liquid surface above the orifice.
This formula incorrectly reverses the order of h1 and h2 and uses g instead of 2g, which does not align with Torricelli’s theorem.
This formula reverses the order of h1 and h2, leading to incorrect application of Torricelli’s theorem.
This formula omits the factor of 2, which is essential in the correct application of Torricelli’s theorem.

questions.explanation: Explanation:Torricelli's theorem states that the velocity (v) of a liquid flowing out of an orifice at the bottom of a container is equal to the velocity acquired by a freely falling body from a height (h1 - h2), where h1 is the height of the liquid surface above the orifice and h2 is the height of the orifice above the ground. Mathematically, it can be expressed as: v2 = √ 2g (h1 - h2)Where g is the acceleration due to gravity.

questions.text: Why should you not stand close to a fast-moving train?
questions.subject: Science Reasoning

options.text: The fast-moving train creates a strong air pressure that can pull you towards it, risking serious injury.
options.explanation: Fast-moving trains generate a significant air pressure difference, leading to a suction effect that can pull people towards the train, posing a serious risk of injury.

options.text: The train's speed might make you feel dizzy, potentially causing you to faint.
options.explanation: Although fast movement can cause dizziness, it is not the primary danger when standing near a fast-moving train. The main risk comes from the air pressure differences.

options.text: Objects falling from the train could hit you due to the train's high velocity, causing injury.
options.explanation: While objects falling from a train can be hazardous, the primary risk is the air pressure difference created by the train's movement.

options.text: There is no real danger; it's just a safety guideline.
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect as it neglects the genuine physical risks posed by standing too close to a fast-moving train due to air pressure differences and potential accidents.

questions.explanation: When you stand close to a fast-moving train, the train's rapid movement creates a lower air pressure between the train and the person compared to the other side. According to Bernoulli's principle, this pressure difference results in a force that can pull a person towards the train, leading to a risk of accidents and injuries. Option A correctly identifies the primary danger associated with standing too close to a fast-moving train. Option B is incorrect because dizziness is not the main concern. Option C is incorrect as the falling objects are not the primary risk. Option D is incorrect because it ignores the real danger posed by air pressure differences.

questions.text: In Bernoulli’s equation, the term 1/2 v2 is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Correct option as explained above
Incorrect form
Incorrect form
Incorrect form

questions.explanation: Bernoulli’s equation formula is a relation between the pressure, kinetic energy, and gravitational potential energy of a fluid in a container. The formula for Bernoulli’s principle is given as follows: p+1/2ρv2+ρgh=constant Where p is the pressure exerted by the fluid, v is the velocity of the fluid, ρ is the density of the fluid, and h is the height of the container. Here 1/2 v2 represents K.E. per unit volume.

questions.text: The blood vessels collapse when:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: External pressure applied becomes greater than the systolic pressure
options.explanation: When external pressure exceeds systolic pressure, the blood vessel collapses because the external force overcomes the pressure inside the vessel.

options.text: External pressure applied is equal to systolic pressure
options.explanation: When the pressures are equal, blood flow is restricted, but the vessel does not fully collapse as the forces are balanced.

options.text: External pressure applied is less than the systolic pressure
options.explanation: If external pressure is less than systolic pressure, the vessel remains open and blood flows normally.
With zero external pressure, there is no opposing force to affect blood flow, so it continues unimpeded.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A. External pressure applied becomes greater than the systolic pressure. This causes the blood vessel to collapse because the external pressure overcomes the internal pressure within the vessel, leading to its closure. Option B is incorrect because when the pressures are equal, the vessel does not collapse but blood flow may be impeded. Option C is incorrect as external pressure less than systolic pressure allows normal blood flow without vessel collapse. Option D is incorrect because zero external pressure does not interfere with the vessel's function, allowing normal circulation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The speed of efflux is equal to the velocity gained by the fluid in falling through the distance (h1-h2) under the action of gravity. Using Bernoulli's equation

questions.text: Water flows through a constriction in horizontal pipe as it enters the constriction, the water's:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Speed increases and pressure remains constant
options.explanation: Pressure decreases, not remains constant.
Pressure decreases, not increases.

When flowing through a constricted area of a pipe, a fluid's velocity increases and its static pressure decreases. This principle is known as the Venturi effect. Fluids speed up when they reach a constriction to maintain a constant volume flow rate.
Speed increases, not decreases.

questions.explanation: We know that the volume flow rate (Avt) is constant, with V/v = A, we know that Area is directionally proportional to velocity. ‘Constriction’ tells us that the area has decreased, thus the speed increases.According to Bernolie’s theorem P + pgh + 1/2 = constant.Value for pgh is constant as it depends on height, the value for 1/2pv2 increases as velocity increases, thus to maintain the constant the value of pressure must decrease.

questions.text: The effect of the decrease in pressure with the increase in speed of the fluid in the horizontal tube gives
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is not correct. Torricelli's effect is related to the flow of fluids through an orifice or small opening, and it involves the relationship between the height of a fluid column and the velocity of the outflow.

Bernoulli's principle states that as the speed of a fluid increases, the pressure exerted by the fluid decreases. This principle applies to the flow of fluids in various situations, including in horizontal tubes. When the fluid flows through a tube and its speed increases, the pressure decreases.

This option is also incorrect. Venturi's effect is a specific application of Bernoulli's principle, which describes the reduction in pressure that occurs when a fluid flows through a constricted section of a pipe.

The Doppler effect refers to the change in frequency or wavelength of a wave due to the relative motion between the source of the wave and the observer. It is not directly related to the pressure and speed of a fluid in a horizontal tube.

questions.explanation: Bernoulli's principle states that as the speed of a fluid increases, the pressure exerted by the fluid decreases. This principle applies to the flow of fluids in various situations, including in horizontal tubes. When the fluid flows through a tube and its speed increases, the pressure decreases.

questions.text: When the drag force is equal to the weight of the droplet, the droplet will fall with:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. If drag force equals weight, it's terminal velocity, balancing forces. High speed implies no drag balance.
Incorrect. Terminal velocity achieved when weight = drag. Low speed doesn't account for balance.
Incorrect. Terminal velocity reached, acceleration stops. Balanced forces negate constant acceleration.

When the downward (weight) and upward (drag) forces are equal in a falling body, the body is said to be in terminal velocity, which means that the body falls with a constant speed since there is no resultant force acting on it.

questions.explanation: When the downward (weight) and upward (drag) forces are equal in a falling body, the body is said to be in terminal velocity, which means that the body falls with a constant speed since there is no resultant force acting on it.

questions.text: A fluid is undergoing incompressible flow which represents that:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Pressure at a given point cannot change with time
options.explanation: Incompressibility does not require pressure to remain constant.Pressure can change with time and from point to point in the fluid — that’s how fluids accelerate or respond to forces.Only density must remain constant, not pressure.Therefore, pressure variation is allowed, so A is wrong.

options.text: Velocity at given point cannot change with time
options.explanation: This statement would describe steady flow, not incompressible flow.In an incompressible flow, velocity may increase or decrease with time if the flow is unsteady.Incompressibility restricts density, not velocity.Thus, B is incorrect because incompressible flows can be steady or unsteady.

options.text: The density cannot change change with time or location
options.explanation: The density cannot change with time or location, since, this directly matches the definition of incompressible flow:𝜌=constantρ=constant everywhere and at all times.

options.text: The velocity must be the same everywhere
options.explanation: This would imply a uniform flow, which is not required at all.Real incompressible flows (like water in a pipe) can have different velocities at different locations.Only density must remain constant; velocity can vary spatially.Therefore, D is wrong because incompressibility does not imply uniform velocity.

questions.explanation: The density cannot change with time or location, since, this directly matches the definition of incompressible flow:𝜌=constantρ=constant everywhere and at all times.

questions.text: A thin horizontal plate of area 0.036 m2 is beneath the surface of a liquid of density 930 kg m−3. The force on one side of the plate due to the pressure of the liquid is 290 N. What is the depth of the plate beneath the surface of the liquid?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct depth. Using the formula for pressure, P = hρg, and knowing that pressure can also be expressed as P = F / A, we can solve for h. Substituting the given values: h = F / (Aρg) = 290 / [0.036 (930) (9.81)] = 0.88 m.
This depth is incorrect. It results from miscalculating the formula or substituting incorrect values in the equation.
This depth is too large. It indicates an error in using the formula or misapplying the values given for force, area, or density.
This depth is excessively large, showing a significant miscalculation in the use of the formula or values.

questions.explanation: Pressure P = hρgwhere h is the depth, ρ is the density of the liquid and g is the acceleration of free fall.But,Pressure P = Force / Area = F / ATherefore,Depth h = P / ρg = F / (Aρg) = 290 / [0.036 (930) (9.81)] = 0.88m

questions.text: The internal energy of the system decreases in an adiabatic process. Which of the following must be true regarding this process?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Heat flows out of the system
options.explanation: In an adiabatic process, there is no heat transfer between the system and its surroundings. Therefore, heat cannot flow out of the system, making this option incorrect.

options.text: Work is done by the system
options.explanation: In an adiabatic process, any change in internal energy is due to work done. If the internal energy decreases, it indicates that the system is doing work on its surroundings. This option is correct.

options.text: Work is done by the surroundings
options.explanation: If work were done by the surroundings, it would typically increase the internal energy of the system. Since the problem states that internal energy decreases, this option is not applicable.

options.text: The potential energy of the system is changing
options.explanation: Potential energy is not directly involved in the context of an adiabatic process affecting internal energy. Adiabatic processes are primarily about work and changes in internal energy, not potential energy.

questions.explanation: According to the first law of thermodynamics, Q = ΔU + W, where Q is the heat added to the system, ΔU is the change in internal energy, and W is the work done by the system. In an adiabatic process, Q = 0, so the equation simplifies to ΔU = -W. This means that if the internal energy decreases, W must be positive, indicating that work is done by the system on the surroundings. Thus, the correct answer is that work is done by the system. The other options are incorrect because they either suggest heat transfer (which does not occur in an adiabatic process) or involve potential energy, which is not directly related to the described process.
